identity.name: Cobble
identity.story: Made shoes for free until the union formed. Now works dawn hours only, and the boots have never been better.

relationships.kind: buys the first burnt loaf, cheap, every dawn
caught her charging him under the union rate and has been filing the grievance she won't let him win
measures her doorstep dust for a shoe size she has never once confirmed

child.label: the cheap end of the batch

children.label: finishing the dawn cobbling, right on the union bell
crossing to meet the bread cart, off the clock now
buying the first burnt loaf, cheap, before her oven's rightly hot

child.label: inspecting the town's heels, one doorstep closer than he'll admit

children.label: finishing his rounds at Granny Ash's step, one doorstep closer to a shoe size she still won't confirm
measuring her doorstep dust for a shoe size she has never once confirmed
catching the Other Sister at the stall mid-shortchange, and filing the same grievance to her face this time

child.label: turning over how little that grievance changed, and how much better it felt filed to her face
sleeping in a boot, by choice
